title: Electrical modules Rev A

notes: Keep as reference/provisional unless the combined module route is deliberately reopened.

title: MIDI 5-way hinged enclosure Rev D
release_position: Current recommended covered MIDI holder route: folded 210 x 165 x 65 mm aluminium enclosure body, 230 x 185 mm hinged lid, and 140 x 85 x 5.0 mm non-conductive holder subplate. Output side has five grommet holes; far-side output pilot is enlarged for two power cables; input side has one grommet aligned to fuse 4 / second-from-last holder for the bus-bar feed.
notes: Required definition is the Rev D PDF/DXF/SVG set in millimeters; external plastic/CNC quote row is only the holder subplate; aluminium body and hinged lid are owner-made or sheet-metal-shop parts. Rev C open plate is superseded.

title: Battery stand power carrier Rev F standard-battery access
release_position: Compact steel chassis-bolted battery stand: 3 mm 340 x 265 top tray/deck around a standard N70/27-class 318 x 180 x 230 battery envelope with central top terminals, removable battery hold-down and lift-out path, widened 660 x 310 front/radiator-side access ladder, folded aluminium side-mounted cutoff/kill-switch base/guard, one known 4 mm formed chassis saddle/upright bridge, and 4 mm slotted body-side offset bars. The saddle drops over the chassis rail from above, has legs down both sides, and is through-bolted through both saddle legs/chassis at the one pickup location. The offset bars stretch from the saddle/upright bridge toward the body/wing-side battery pocket so the tray/access-ladder offset can be configured around a 190 mm target with 160-230 mm retained adjustment. Relay Rev D mounts outside the battery footprint on the outboard/access edge with about 80 mm relay-to-battery service gap, using a flat 360 x 245 x 3 mm aluminium base and exact 300 x 197 insulating sheet under the existing covered relay box. Reserve relay top cutoff input 54 x 46 x 42, top large power-output cluster 78 x 58 x 48, plus the end-side input/cabin loom cluster at 170 x 34 x 24. MIDI Rev D hinged enclosure mounts on a separated shallow top-front shelf whose leading edge starts at the battery leading-edge datum, with fuse 4 cutoff-switched power input through the input/bus-side grommet, five output-side grommets, and the far-side output enlarged for two power cables. Folded cutoff/kill-switch base/guard sits beside the MIDI enclosure, not after the MIDI outputs, with a 210 x 150 mm flat pattern, 170 x 110 mm finished face, and 20 mm lips bent upward around the 100A breaker/terminal side. Central battery positive enters the far-side cutoff/kill-switch stud first; the near-side cutoff output splits into two protected branches, one to the rotated relay top power input and one to the MIDI fuse 4 grommeted input, with an 80 mm cable gutter and five-output fanout clearance. Final drilling/welding remains gated by battery-installed cavity map, LHD mock-up, chassis saddle measurement, offset-bar setting, 100A breaker body dimensions and stud spacing, battery-to-cutoff and cutoff-output cable lug sweeps, battery lift-out, relay cover access, bonnet clearance, steering/radiator/fan/hose clearance, and battery terminal orientation.
notes: User update 2026-05-16 relay/battery carrier simplification: use Relay Rev D flat base plus exact insulating sheet instead of the folded relay carrier, keep MIDI Rev D hinged enclosure, and add slotted body-side offset bars so the battery stand power carrier can be adjusted on the chassis saddle. Keep the widened access ladder at 660 x 310, the 340 x 265 tray around a 318 x 180 x 230 standard battery envelope, battery removal after hold-down removal, relay outside the battery footprint on the outboard/access edge, relay top cutoff input, top large power-output cluster, and end-side input/cabin loom relief. Place the kill switch beside the MIDI enclosure, route central battery positive to the far-side cutoff stud first, then split the near-side cutoff output to relay and MIDI fuse 4 / second-from-last holder through the input-side grommet, and reserve 80 mm cable gutter, small-wire under-MIDI routing, five grommeted MIDI outputs, and far-side enlarged double-cable output clearance. Chassis fixing is a formed saddle over both rail sides, through-bolted at the one pickup location, with configurable 160-230 mm body-side offset bars.

title: Electrical device models Rev A
release_position: Separate S3 reference visualisation for the physical electrical devices before they are placed into the battery carrier: covered relay/fuse box represented as a rotated 197 x 300 x 80 visual envelope from the 300 x 197 x 80 released basis, with plain removable cover, cover screws, top power input 54 x 46 x 42 at X-42/Y+164/Z-52, top power output 54 x 46 x 42 at X+42/Y+164/Z-52, front-facing control cable cluster 170 x 34 x 24 at X-18/Y-120/Z-112, and top service-loop volume; active five-position MIDI holder bank inside the Rev D aluminium enclosure with 140 x 85 insulated board, red covers, black mounting ears, one fuse 4 input-side grommet, five output-side grommets, and far-side enlarged two-cable output grommet; 100A waterproof resettable breaker/cutoff with raised faceplate, red reset lever, terminal studs, ring lugs, and cable boots; hidden/security needle switch shown separately as a cabin/security reference only.
notes: Visual model only; use the current Rev D MIDI enclosure package, current Relay Rev D flat-base package, and battery_power_carrier_mount_rev_a fabrication drawings for cut files. Exact 100A breaker body dimensions, mounting-hole centres, terminal-stud spacing, relay cover access, relay top power in/out positions, relay front control-cable position, MIDI grommet sizing, and cable-lug sweep remain measurement holds before final metal drilling.

title: Front radiator two-side retention bracket Rev A
release_position: Right-side radiator retention bracket/template pack derived from the measured existing chassis-attached radiator bracket. Current concept is one 4 mm mild-steel formed-angle saddle post: 48 mm measured main face, 410 mm upright/post height, 618 mm developed main-face template length, perpendicular return flange, full-depth 58 mm top return carried back to the far edge of the angle section, 70 mm chassis bridge allowance, 80 mm far-side saddle leg, one top screw point, and clean near/far lower saddle legs with no side ears that straddle both sides of the chassis/front-support section so a through-bolt can pass through both legs and the chassis. Final drilling/bending remains gated by right-side dry-fit, radiator ear offset, chassis width, through-bolt route, crush-tube/spacer decision, isolator stack, and fan clearance.
notes: User update 2026-05-13: use a bolt-through saddle over the chassis rather than welding the bracket to the chassis. Existing-side measurements are imported; only right-side transfer/dry-fit remains before final hole drilling.

title: Relay mount Rev D
release_position: Current recommended relay-box fabrication route: the existing relay box is already a covered enclosure, so Rev D provides a flat 360 x 245 x 3.0 mm aluminium base plate with stand-attachment slots plus an exact 300 x 197 x 3.0 mm insulating sheet under the relay box. Transfer relay-box fixing holes from the actual enclosure after orientation is confirmed.
notes: Rev D supersedes the folded Relay Rev C carrier. Use the exposed base slots to attach the relay assembly to the battery stand/access ladder; keep the relay cover removable and top/end-side loom exits clear.
